title: Keep MCP WebRTC data channel after signaling close
description: Follow-up production issue after txing-68z: the browser briefly reports MCP over WebRTC data channel, then immediately falls back to MQTT while the video WebRTC peer remains connected. Scope: keep the established MCP WebRTC data channel alive after normal KVS signaling websocket close/error events, avoid fallback oscillation, preserve MQTT fallback for pre-open failures, and verify web/native/board tests.

title: Throttle browser KVS video reconnect attempts
description: Production regression after txing-0s8: when a board reaches REDCON 1 and the detail panel opens, browser video/MCP setup can produce KVS throttling and repeated MCP session-not-initialized runtime errors. Scope: prevent browser reconnect/remount loops from hammering KVS control-plane APIs, make WebRTC MCP initialization robust across the browser and native board bridge, preserve MQTT fallback behavior and lease semantics, and add a visible web indicator for the active MCP transport.

title: Phase 4 web MCP transport selection
description: Rework web MCP client to use a transport abstraction with WebRTC data channel preferred when advertised and healthy, and MQTT as mandatory fallback for every MCP device.
design: Share the KVS peer connection used by video; create txing.mcp.v1 before the offer; fall back to MQTT on setup/open/request failure; clear MCP state on active WebRTC failure before reacquiring over MQTT.

title: Phase 4 native KVS MCP data channel bridge
description: Add data-channel support to the in-tree KVS master: accept txing.mcp.v1, forward UTF-8 JSON-RPC messages to board IPC, send responses back via dataChannelSend, and notify IPC on channel/session close.
design: If no IPC socket path is provided, preserve video-only behavior. Keep the existing video KVS channel and do not add another signaling channel.

title: Phase 4 board MCP transport-neutral core and IPC
description: Refactor board MCP service so JSON-RPC handling is transport-neutral, keep MQTT adapter behavior unchanged, add per-session close handling, and add a Unix domain socket server owned by txing-board for WebRTC MCP IPC.
design: The IPC server forwards text JSON-RPC payloads into the same MCP server core and returns response frames. Closing a transport session stops motion only if that session owns the lease.

title: Phase 4 MCP descriptor transports contract
description: Extend shared MCP descriptor payloads and parsers to advertise ordered transports while preserving legacy MQTT fields and MQTT-only compatibility.
design: Add webrtc-datachannel descriptor fields only when board video/WebRTC MCP is enabled; always include mqtt-jsonrpc for MCP devices. Update shared Python and web tests.

title: Phase 4 MCP WebRTC data channel with MQTT fallback
description:
Implement Phase 4 from the approved plan: MCP, video, and WebRTC are separate optional capabilities; MQTT remains the mandatory fallback whenever MCP exists; devices with MCP plus the existing video KVS channel prefer MCP over a WebRTC data channel on that channel; no separate MCP signaling channel is added; current MCP tools and lease semantics remain unchanged.

Public interface intent: retain legacy MQTT descriptor fields and add an ordered transports array containing webrtc-datachannel when available and mqtt-jsonrpc always for MCP devices. Sparkplug remains the compact summary via descriptorTopic; Thing Shadow is not extended for MCP.

Implementation scope: transport-neutral board MCP core, MQTT adapter preservation, board-local Unix socket IPC for native KVS data-channel bridge, web MCP transport selection with WebRTC preferred and MQTT fallback, shared browser realtime session reuse, and focused tests for descriptor compatibility, board session handling, native data-channel bridge, and web fallback behavior.
design: Reuse the existing <device_id>-board-video KVS channel for the WebRTC data channel. Use label txing.mcp.v1. Security hardening beyond existing AWS access plus MCP lease gate is out of scope. Active leases are not migrated across transports; transport failure clears client MCP state and reacquires over fallback. Leave Beads tasks open through review/commit.

title: Fast-path wake command during sleep rendezvous
description: When a sleeping device advertises briefly, the rig performs full GATT service discovery, notification setup, and state sync before writing a wake command. The MCU often disconnects during that setup, so wake commands are delayed across many rendezvous attempts. After a wake write succeeds, missing confirmation can also keep the target pending and cause repeated reconnect churn.
design: Send wake as soon as the BLE connection is established when a non-4 REDCON target is pending, before normal session sync. After a successful wake write, accept the wake transition if later confirmation is unavailable, mirroring the existing REDCON 4 post-sleep behavior. Add regression coverage for fast wake ordering and wake confirmation fallback.

title: Restore Beads auto-export same-commit close workflow
description: Re-enable default Beads export behavior in this repo and change the custom git hook flow so Beads-Close trailers close issues, export .beads/issues.jsonl, and amend the just-created commit so issue state and code land in the same final commit. Update repo instructions to describe the amended post-commit flow and any caveats.

title: Do not treat stale reported_power as wake convergence when BLE is offline
description: After a REDCON 4 sleep transition, the rig can retain reported_power=true from the pre-sleep session while BLE presence later drops to offline. A subsequent wake command is then ignored because the wake path returns early on reported_power=true even though redcon is still 4 and BLE is disconnected.
design: Require BLE online before the wake path treats reported_power=true as sufficient, and add a regression test that covers wake-after-sleep with stale reported_power plus BLE offline.

title: Keep BLE management independent from REDCON sleep state
description: After REDCON 4 convergence, the rig still intentionally disconnects BLE and idles disconnected while the MCU reports sleep. That breaks wake-sleep-wake because the next DCMD.redcon transition has no active BLE recovery loop behind it, and the logs do not show command receipt clearly enough to debug.
design: Remove the intentional BLE disconnect/idle-sleep policy from the rig bridge so reconnect attempts continue regardless of reported sleep state, and add explicit logs when Sparkplug DCMD.redcon is received and applied.

title: Restart shared scanner before fleet reconnect waits
description: RigFleetBridge stops the shared scanner before connect, but after an unexpected BLE disconnect the next reconnect attempt can wait for a fresh advertisement without first restarting the scanner. In practice the rig falls into repeated 'matched advertisement not seen before connect deadline' loops and REDCON 4 can no longer converge because BLE never reconnects.
design: Ensure fleet reconnect paths restart the shared scanner before any advertisement wait, and add a regression test that simulates an unexpected disconnect followed by reconnect candidate selection.

title: Add Sparkplug subscribe permission to device runtime role
description: Board now subscribes to the Sparkplug device command topic for REDCON 4 shutdown, but the device runtime IAM policy still lacks iot:Subscribe/iot:Receive on spBv1.0/* topics. AWS IoT drops the MQTT connection during startup subscribe, and the board loops on AWS_ERROR_MQTT_CANCELLED_FOR_CLEAN_SESSION.
design: Add Sparkplug topic and topicfilter permissions to the device runtime managed policy, and extend the template policy tests so the board-required permissions are covered explicitly.

title: Fix board MQTT bootstrap clean-session retry
description: Board startup retries after shadow topic subscribe failures, but the failed MQTT session is not fully torn down before the next clean-session reconnect attempt. In practice this can loop on AWS_ERROR_MQTT_CANCELLED_FOR_CLEAN_SESSION at startup even though the outer retry delay is already applied.
design: Track the in-progress board MQTT client before subscriptions finish, wait for the failed session to close after disconnect, and add a regression test that reproduces a subscribe failure which only clears after the previous connection closed callback fires.

title: Fix board runtime DescribeThing IAM permission
description: Board startup now resolves its Sparkplug assignment by calling IoT DescribeThing on the registered device thing. The device runtime IAM role currently allows GetThingShadow and DescribeEndpoint but not DescribeThing, which causes board.service startup to fail with AccessDeniedException under the town-device-runtime assumed role.
design: Update the stack-managed device runtime policy to allow iot:DescribeThing on device things, add an aws.check device-scope probe for DescribeThing, and cover the policy/check behavior in tests so role-based board bring-up fails early in validation instead of at service startup.

title: Refactor web catalog and UI for reported-only hierarchy
description: Remove desired.redcon UI logic, read device state from reported.device.*, switch catalog labels to name, and make discovery use the hierarchical town/rig/device AWS structure instead of the old rig-group heuristics.
design: Command acknowledgement stays reported-only: the browser keeps a local pending target and clears it only when the observed primary redcon reaches the commanded state.

title: Switch board shutdown to Sparkplug DCMD redcon
description: Remove board shutdown control via shadow desired fields, resolve board assignment from registry at startup, subscribe the board to its Sparkplug device-command topic, and reuse the existing local halt path when DCMD.redcon=4 arrives.
design: Board ignores DCMD.redcon=1..3, keeps best-effort shutdown reporting under reported.device.board.*, and does not introduce any new internal MQTT control topic.

title: Make rig runtime and device shadows reported-only
description: Remove desired-state lifecycle handling from the rig runtime, keep target redcon in memory only, move device battery/mcu/board data under reported.device.*, and keep device lifecycle convergence driven solely by Sparkplug commands plus observed board/MCU state.
design: Hard break: no legacy desired parsing, no restart persistence of pending lifecycle intent, and no compatibility support for old shadow shapes after manual cutover.

title: Refactor AWS hierarchy for town/rig/device things
description: Add town and rig AWS IoT thing support, normalize registry attributes to name/shortId, keep device thing type as the only type source, create town dynamic groups for rigs, keep rig dynamic groups for devices, and add the AWS-side rig lifecycle reflector and stack/indexing changes needed for the hard-break hierarchy.
design: Final-stage only: no migration command. Manual cutover will rebuild the AWS stack and recreate town/rig/device registry objects in the new structure.

title: Hard-break reported-only txing hierarchy
description: Implement the reported-only shadow hierarchy across device, rig, and town. Remove all desired-state lifecycle handling, move device detail under reported.device.*, promote town and rig to first-class AWS IoT things with name/shortId attributes and <type>-<shortId> thing names, keep Sparkplug as the only lifecycle command transport, and deliver the final-stage contract with manual cutover instructions instead of migration tooling.
design: Scope includes shared AWS registry/stack updates, rig lifecycle/runtime refactor, board Sparkplug shutdown subscription, web catalog/UI updates, tests, and documentation. Hard-break assumptions: no compatibility support for legacy shadows or legacy registry attributes after cutover; town stays reported.redcon=1; board shuts down on DCMD.redcon=4 from Sparkplug and does not use shadow desired fields.

title: Fix strict MCP teleop request timeout and pulsing
description: After the Phase 3 strict MCP cutover, holding teleop up/down can show long start delay, repeated stop/restart behavior, and browser runtime errors like "Timed out waiting for MCP response to tools/call". Investigate the web MCP request hot path against the board MCP service and fix the control loop so steady teleop stays responsive without request/response stalls or watchdog-like pulsing.

title: Collapse rig Sparkplug edge id to rig identity
description:
Web publishes REDCON DCMD to spBv1.0/<town>/DCMD/<route rig>/<device>, but the rig runtime still allows Sparkplug edge node id to diverge from the rig thing-group name through service env overrides. When those drift, DCMD never reaches rig, desired.redcon never reflects into shadow, and wake commands appear to succeed in the web UI while nothing happens on the rig.

Fix by making rig Sparkplug edge node id derive from rig identity in runtime, logging when a distinct override is ignored, and updating docs/tests so route rig and Sparkplug edge are a single identity.

title: Strict MCP teleop pulses instead of staying continuous
description: Phase 3 regression. During continuous teleop in strict MCP mode, the board alternates between moving and stopping every few seconds instead of maintaining continuous motion. Investigate web MCP command cadence versus board cmd_vel watchdog timing and fix without reintroducing raw MQTT fallback.

title: Strict MCP teleop lease expires during steady motion
description: Phase 3 regression. In strict MCP teleop, the web client can let the board control lease expire during continuous motion, causing board stop and a runtime error 'No active control lease'. Fix the web MCP client to proactively refresh leases during steady motion and recover from recoverable lease expiry/token errors by reacquiring and retrying the motion command once. Keep MCP as the only remote control API; do not reintroduce raw MQTT cmd_vel fallback.

title: Rig and shadow cleanup for strict MCP
description:
Rig and shadow cleanup for Phase 3.

Stop reflecting top-level state.reported.video into shadow. Remove state.reported.board.drive and state.reported.video from the shadow schema/defaults/code paths while keeping REDCON derivation from retained MCP and video readiness unchanged. No runtime cleanup or delete-on-write compatibility logic; shadow reset is manual after deploy.

title: Web strict MCP client cutover
description:
Web strict MCP client cutover.

Remove all raw cmd_vel publish and fallback behavior. Use MCP session initialization, lease acquire/renew/release, cmd_vel.publish/cmd_vel.stop, and robot.get_state as the only live board control/read path. Keep Sparkplug REDCON as the lifecycle gate.

title: Board strict MCP cutover
description:
Board strict MCP cutover.

Add robot.get_state to the board-hosted MCP server, make cmd_vel.publish and cmd_vel.stop return applied motion state, and remove the raw MQTT <device>/board/cmd_vel remote control path. CmdVelController remains internal and is fed only from MCP tools. Keep retained MCP and video descriptor/status topics for discovery and lifecycle inputs.

title: [EPIC] Phase 3 strict MCP board control
description:
Phase 3 strict MCP cutover.

Implement a clean architecture where MCP is the only remote board control API. Remove the direct MQTT <device>/board/cmd_vel path entirely. Keep lifecycle ownership with rig and Sparkplug. Add robot.get_state as the AI-friendly read tool. Remove replaced shadow fields from schema and code path: state.reported.board.drive and top-level state.reported.video. Do not add compatibility cleanup code for old shadow documents; shadow will be reset manually after deploy.

title: Authorize rig video MQTT topics in AWS IoT policy
description:
Rig now subscribes to txings/<device-id>/video/descriptor and txings/<device-id>/video/status, but the AWS IoT policy template only authorizes MCP topic subscribe/receive for the rig/runtime role.

Observed behavior:
- MQTT connect succeeds
- first subscribe to unauthorized topic likely causes immediate broker disconnect/hangup
- rig loops in bootstrap with AWS_ERROR_MQTT_UNEXPECTED_HANGUP and CANCELLED_FOR_CLEAN_SESSION follow-on errors

Scope:
- add video descriptor/status subscribe/receive permissions alongside MCP in the AWS template
- revert broad txings wildcard bootstrap subscription back to exact authorized topics
- add check/test coverage where practical

title: Reduce rig startup subscribe fan-out to shorten MQTT bootstrap
description:
Rig startup now performs more sequential MQTT subscribe operations before completing initial shadow bootstrap. The WebSocket is hanging up during that longer bootstrap window.

Scope:
- reduce the number of subscribe calls in AwsShadowClient startup by using broader topic filters where safe
- preserve current message handling semantics
- add regression coverage for the new topic filters if practical

title: Back off rig shadow bootstrap after MQTT unexpected hangups
description:
The rig shadow bootstrap now survives clean-session subscribe cancellation, but repeated AWS MQTT unexpected hangups during bootstrap can still cause a tight reconnect loop.

Observed behavior:
- connect succeeds
- websocket hangs up during bootstrap
- bootstrap retries immediately several times
- eventually connect itself raises AWS_ERROR_MQTT_UNEXPECTED_HANGUP and outer startup retry triggers

Scope:
- treat unexpected hangup as retryable during initial shadow bootstrap
- add reconnect-delay backoff inside bootstrap retry loop
- add regression coverage for a transient unexpected-hangup bootstrap failure

title: Retry rig shadow bootstrap when clean-session reconnect cancels subscribe
description:
The rig shadow bootstrap can fail repeatedly even though the service now retries startup.

Observed behavior:
- MQTT connect succeeds
- subscribe during AwsShadowClient.connect/connect_and_get_initial_snapshots raises AWS_ERROR_MQTT_CANCELLED_FOR_CLEAN_SESSION
- startup loop logs Rig startup failed and restarts the whole client

Scope:
- make AwsShadowClient bootstrap resilient to clean-session reconnects during initial subscribe/get
- retry bootstrap inside the cloud shadow client instead of failing immediately
- add regression coverage around bootstrap retry

title: Retry rig startup after transient AWS IoT connect interruption
description:
The rig service exits if AWS IoT MQTT over WebSocket is interrupted during the initial connect/subscription/snapshot phase before the fleet bridge run loop starts.

Observed failure:
- connect succeeds
- WebSocket is interrupted during initial subscribe/get
- awscrt raises AWS_ERROR_MQTT_CANCELLED_FOR_CLEAN_SESSION
- exception escapes _run_rig before the existing while True retry loop begins
- systemd restarts the service

Scope:
- make startup resilient to transient AWS IoT interruptions during connect_and_get_initial_snapshots
- retry after reconnect_delay instead of exiting the process
- add a regression test around the startup retry path if practical

title: Prevent MCP teleop watchdog oscillation under RTT
description: Board teleop still oscillates between motion and watchdog stop when browser control uses per-command MCP RPC round trips over higher-latency links. Current web runtime serializes every non-zero cmd_vel.publish behind request/response completion, so effective publish cadence can exceed the 0.5s board watchdog and produce repeated 20/0/20/0 drive transitions. Scope: keep MCP session + lease management, but make steady-state motion delivery watchdog-safe under realistic MQTT RTT and add regression coverage for the web runtime behavior.
design: Investigate the current web MCP teleop path against txing-f42. Keep MCP initialize/acquire/renew/release semantics, but avoid making continuous motion depend on a request/response RTT for every tick. Prefer a latest-state path that can keep board watchdog fed while the lease stays valid, and cover the intended behavior with focused tests.

title: Stabilize web MCP teleop lease recovery
description: Web teleop over MCP becomes inconsistent under lease expiry or moderate MQTT round-trip latency. Current runtime serializes every periodic cmd_vel publish into an unbounded FIFO and only treats invalid lease token as recoverable during renewal. This causes stale motion commands, abrupt watchdog stops, raw topic fallback, and oscillating motion after several seconds of teleop. Scope: make cmd_vel publishing latest-state driven, recover from no-active-lease responses by reacquiring, and add regression coverage.
design: Keep the current stepped teleop semantics. Fix the browser shadow runtime so only the latest desired twist is sent after each in-flight publish completes instead of queueing every tick. Treat MCP lease loss as recoverable in renew/publish paths by clearing local lease state and reacquiring before retrying. Verify with web tests.

title: web: prevent MCP lease churn from clock-skewed expiresAt timestamps
description:
Scope:
- Stabilize MCP cmd_vel lease renewal by avoiding reliance on server wall-clock expiresAtMs for client renewal decisions.
- Use locally-derived lease expiry from leaseTtlMs to avoid browser/board clock skew causing premature expiry and stop/reacquire loops.

Acceptance:
- During sustained teleop, board logs do not show periodic drive 0 pulses caused by lease expiry churn.
- MCP path remains active without repeated reconnect/reinitialize cycles under normal network conditions.
- Web tests/build remain green.
notes: Switched web MCP lease expiry tracking to local Date.now()+leaseTtlMs instead of server expiresAtMs to avoid board/browser wall-clock skew causing lease churn (periodic stop/reacquire behavior). Web tests + build pass.

title: mcp: preserve session initialization across lease churn and show MCP status in panel
description:
Scope:
- Fix board MCP lifecycle so lease release/expiry does not force a full session re-initialize for the same MQTT session.
- Add client-side recovery when MCP reports session-not-initialized during cmd_vel flow.
- Add visible MCP connected/disconnected indicator near the video control button.

Acceptance:
- No repeated fallback with 'MCP session is not initialized' during normal teleop for an active session.
- Txing panel shows MCP connection state indicator near video button.
- Board + web tests and web build pass.

title: web: serialize MCP cmd_vel lease usage to prevent invalid lease token fallback
description:
Scope:
- Prevent overlapping MCP cmd_vel calls from racing lease renew/acquire and using stale lease tokens.
- Keep MCP as primary cmd_vel path without frequent fallback to legacy raw topic.

Approach:
- Serialize cmd_vel publish operations through a per-session queue in web shadow runtime.
- On MCP invalid lease token errors, clear cached lease and reacquire once before failing.

Acceptance:
- Teleop no longer repeatedly logs 'Invalid lease token' fallback under normal key-repeat usage.
- Web tests and build remain green.

title: board: avoid MCP session response timeout by offloading MCP handling from MQTT callback thread
description:
Scope:
- Fix board MCP request handling so MQTT inbound callback thread does not block on synchronous response publish/ack waits.
- Ensure MCP initialize/tools responses are reliably published to txings/<device>/mcp/session/*/s2c under load.
- Improve MCP response publish warning logging to include actionable error details.

Acceptance:
- Web MCP initialize no longer times out due to callback-thread blocking.
- board logs do not show periodic 10s MCP response publish timeouts for active sessions under nominal connectivity.
- Board unit tests pass.

title: aws: grant IoT policy access for txings/<device-id>/mcp topic family
description:
Scope:
- Update shared/aws/template.yaml IAM/IoT policy statements to allow board, rig, and web principals to use MCP MQTT topics under txings/<device-id>/mcp/...
- Keep existing legacy <device>/board/cmd_vel permissions unchanged for compatibility.

Required topic patterns:
- txings/*/mcp/descriptor
- txings/*/mcp/status
- txings/*/mcp/session/*/c2s
- txings/*/mcp/session/*/s2c

Acceptance:
- Board runtime can publish retained descriptor/status and subscribe c2s + publish s2c.
- Rig runtime can subscribe/receive retained descriptor/status.
- Web identities can publish c2s and subscribe/receive s2c and retained descriptor/status.
- CloudFormation template remains syntactically valid.
notes:
Patched shared/aws/template.yaml to add MCP MQTT topic permissions for rig runtime managed policy, device runtime managed policy, town-rig-device IoT policy, web-admin IoT policy, and authenticated identity pool inline policy. New topic family: txings/*/mcp/{descriptor,status,session/*/{c2s,s2c}}.
Reduced web IoT policy size by collapsing shadow/sparkplug/mcp permissions into compact wildcard statements and removing redundant SparkplugCommandPublish/Mcp* blocks in TxingWebAdminIotPolicy and authenticated-role inline policy. Rough serialized sizes (sanitized parse): TxingWebAdminIotPolicy=1179 chars, inline IAM policy=1764 chars.
Further reduced policy size using coarse wildcards per user request. TxingWebAdminIotPolicy now only has 3 statements: Connect client/*, Publish+Receive topic/*, Subscribe topicfilter/*. Mirrored same coarse IoT wildcard model in authenticated identity role inline policy.
Identified actual size offender as TxingGatewayPolicy (2421 chars). Compressed TxingGatewayPolicy to broad wildcard messaging permissions (topic/*, topicfilter/*, client/*) while keeping ThingShadow Get/Update. New estimated sizes: TxingGatewayPolicy=679, TxingWebAdminIotPolicy=429, authenticated-role inline policy=1083.
Likely root cause for board MQTT connect hangup: retained MCP will/status publishes require iot:RetainPublish, not just iot:Publish. Added iot:RetainPublish to DeviceMcpTopics, TxingGatewayPolicy MessagingPublishReceive, TxingWebAdminIotPolicy publish/receive block, and authenticated-role inline IoTPublishReceive. Policy sizes remain under IoT limit (Gateway 699, Web 449).

title: rig/: integrate per-device MCP discovery metrics into Sparkplug publication model
description:
Integrate MCP discovery metrics into the Sparkplug model for the selected device.

Scope:
- Implement the chosen Sparkplug publication model for `services/mcp/*`.
- Ensure the selected `<town>/<rig>/<device-id>` Sparkplug session exposes MCP discovery and availability in the agreed way.
- Keep lifecycle authority and existing lifecycle semantics coherent with the new MCP discovery surface.

Constraints:
- This work depends directly on the Sparkplug authority decision.
- Preserve current lifecycle authority unless explicitly changed by the decision.
- Keep device identity aligned with the route-selected AWS IoT thing name / stable `device_id`.

Intended approach:
- Apply the decision outcome cleanly rather than introducing an implicit hybrid model.
- Update Sparkplug metric publication with explicit ownership and session rules.

title: web/: consume per-device mcp over MQTT/WSS on route-selected device pages
description:
Add MCP client behavior to the route-driven web SPA.

Scope:
- Discover `services/mcp/*` for the route-selected `<town>/<rig>/<device>` device.
- Fetch the retained descriptor from `txings/<device-id>/mcp/descriptor`.
- Open and initialize an MCP session over the existing AWS IoT MQTT/WSS connection.
- Acquire and renew the control lease during teleop.
- Replace direct raw `cmd_vel` publish as the intended long-term control path.

Constraints:
- Preserve the route-driven model delivered under txing-5jn.
- Keep canonical browser routes derived from route schema.
- Do not start until the Sparkplug authority decision is resolved.

Intended approach:
- Integrate MCP client behavior only on route-selected device detail flows.
- Reuse the existing authenticated browser MQTT session rather than introducing a second transport stack.

title: board/: host per-device mcp server and lease-controlled cmd_vel tools
description:
Implement the board-hosted MCP server described in Phase 2.

Scope:
- Host the `mcp` server on the board runtime.
- Publish retained MCP descriptor and status topics under `txings/<device-id>/mcp/...`.
- Expose MCP session topics and implement tools `control.acquire_lease`, `control.renew_lease`, `control.release_lease`, `cmd_vel.publish`, and `cmd_vel.stop`.
- Enforce lease ownership and stop motion on expiry, disconnect, or explicit release.
- Route `cmd_vel.publish` into the existing board motion pipeline.

Constraints:
- Follow the phase-2 design doc.
- Keep Thing Shadow out of MCP discovery/runtime state.
- Do not treat derived browser URLs as board-owned facts.
- Do not start until the Sparkplug authority decision is resolved.

Intended approach:
- Reuse existing board MQTT connectivity where possible.
- Keep the first MCP surface small and focused on motion control.

title: shared/: add per-device MCP-over-MQTT transport and retained descriptor primitives
description:
Build the shared protocol and MQTT helper layer for Phase 2 MCP.

Scope:
- Add builders/parsers for the per-device MCP namespace `txings/<device-id>/mcp/...`.
- Define JSON-RPC session topic helpers for `descriptor`, `status`, `session/{sessionId}/c2s`, and `session/{sessionId}/s2c`.
- Add retained publish support needed for MCP descriptor/status topics.
- Add any shared MCP transport helpers needed by both board and web.

Constraints:
- Follow the saved phase-2 design doc.
- Keep browser URLs and video URLs derived from route schema, not encoded as canonical descriptor URLs.
- Do not assume a final Sparkplug publication model until the decision issue is resolved.

Intended approach:
- Put cross-cutting topic and transport helpers in shared/protocol locations rather than duplicating logic in board and web.
- Keep the API keyed by `<device-id>` and not by a hardcoded thing name.

title: architecture: resolve Sparkplug authority for per-device MCP discovery metrics
description:
Resolve the open Sparkplug authority question captured in `docs/phase-2-txing-mcp.md` before implementation starts.

Scope:
- Decide whether `board` may publish MCP-related metrics directly into the existing `<town>/<rig>/<device-id>` Sparkplug session or whether `rig` remains the sole publisher for the selected Sparkplug device.
- Define DBIRTH/DDATA ownership for MCP discovery metrics.
- Define sequence ownership, rebirth behavior, disconnect handling, and stale handling for the chosen publication model.
- Define metric ownership boundaries between `rig` lifecycle metrics and MCP service-discovery metrics.

Constraints:
- Keep Sparkplug authoritative for lifecycle management unless an explicit replacement decision is made.
- Preserve the existing route-driven town/rig/device identity model.
- Do not begin feature implementation until this decision is recorded.

Intended approach:
- Compare at least these models: board co-publish into the existing device session, rig mirrors board state into Sparkplug, or a separate Sparkplug identity for board publication.
- Record the chosen model and update the phase-2 design doc accordingly.
notes: Decision resolved: board publishes retained MQTT MCP state under txings/<device-id>/mcp/..., rig subscribes to that state, and rig remains the only publisher of the selected <town>/<rig>/<device-id> Sparkplug device session. Sparkplug discovery metrics for MCP are mirrored by rig; shadow is not part of the MCP status path.

title: Phase 2: expose per-device MCP remote API over MQTT
description:
Approved implementation summary:
- Add a board-hosted MCP server named `mcp`.
- Use the per-device MQTT namespace `txings/<device-id>/mcp/...`.
- Keep the web app route-driven at `/<town>/<rig>/<device>` and have it consume MCP over the already established AWS IoT MQTT/WSS session.
- Use Sparkplug as the discovery and availability summary surface for MCP and a retained MQTT descriptor topic for detailed transport/session metadata.
- Keep Thing Shadow out of MCP discovery and MCP runtime state.
- Require a short-lived control lease for `cmd_vel`.

Scope:
- board-hosted MCP server and lease-controlled cmd_vel tools
- shared MQTT/MCP transport helpers
- web MCP client integration on route-selected device pages
- rig Sparkplug integration for MCP discovery metrics
- follow-up documentation updates once the Sparkplug authority decision is resolved

Constraints:
- Do not start implementation work until the Sparkplug authority question is resolved.
- Preserve the route-driven identity model delivered under epic txing-5jn.
- Preserve derived browser URL rules: canonical device/video routes stay route-derived, not descriptor-owned URLs.
- Treat `device` as the AWS IoT thing name / stable `device_id`.

Open architectural point:
- It is not yet decided whether services other than `rig`, specifically `board`, may publish additional MCP-related metrics into the existing `<rig>` / `<device-id>` Sparkplug session.
- This remains unresolved because shared Sparkplug publication across separate services and machines requires explicit decisions for DBIRTH/DDATA ownership, sequence ownership, rebirth behavior, disconnect handling, stale handling, and metric ownership boundaries.

Primary design reference:
- docs/phase-2-txing-mcp.md

title: Retheme web shell to match txing.dev and simplify home link
description: Refine the web shell so the navigation logo routes to the app root instead of the marketing site, the signed-out screen uses the txing logo and TXING label instead of Bot, and the shell colors align with the live txing.dev marketing palette: dark glass panels, cyan accent, and muted light text. Keep the existing route model and device controls intact while updating the surrounding shell presentation.

title: Simplify web navigation line and catalog headings
description: Refine the drilldown layout so the navigation panel becomes a single line with logo, TXING wordmark, and breadcrumb path only. Remove the extra large title from the navigation panel and remove redundant 'Available ...' headings from the town and rig catalog pages so those pages present the rig/device cards directly. Keep the existing route model and card presentation from the current drilldown implementation.

title: Refine rig screen device labels and navigation sizing
description: Adjust the rig and device drilldown presentation so the rig screen uses the same heading pattern as the town screen, device cards show deviceName instead of device_id when available, and the navigation panel matches the device panel width/visual footprint. Remove redundant device identifiers from the rig catalog body while preserving route-driven selection by stable device_id.

title: Refine town screen header and rig cards
description: Adjust the town route layout so the navigation panel presents the logo followed by big TXING and the selected town. Remove redundant town text from the rig list body. Render rigs as cards rather than link rows, with the rig name in uppercase and the description sourced from the AWS IoT thing group description. Keep the work within the existing web drilldown architecture.

title: Simplify rig and town drilldown links
description:
Adjust the drilldown list presentation on town and rig pages to read like clear links instead of pill-style horizontal buttons.

Scope:
- render town and rig drilldown entries as clear text-link rows
- use a schema like <name>: <description> for rig and related list items
- keep the first phase list pages attribute-light while improving readability and scanability

Constraint:
- stay consistent with the current route-driven SPA and do not add heavy metadata cards or extra controls in this pass
design: The current list/button treatment feels like controls instead of navigation. Replace it with clearer link-style rows that communicate drilldown intent.
notes: Implemented by commits 475e2f3 and 39adea1. Town and rig drilldown presentation was simplified from pill-style controls into clearer navigation-oriented list/card presentation.

title: Use deviceName in device drilldown breadcrumbs
description:
Use AWS IoT registry deviceName metadata when presenting the selected device in the web drilldown UI.

Scope:
- show the selected device as its deviceName instead of the raw device_id where that improves navigation clarity
- update the breadcrumb path to read town -> rig -> bot for the current unit device
- preserve device_id as the runtime identity for shadow, cmd_vel, and route segments

Constraint:
- if registry metadata is unavailable, fall back cleanly to device_id without breaking navigation or loading
design: The route continues to use stable device_id, but the visible breadcrumb label should prefer deviceName from thing attributes so the UI reads like bot instead of unit-kiv3mj.
notes: Implemented by commits 475e2f3 and 39adea1. The drilldown UI now prefers deviceName metadata for the selected device label while preserving device_id as the stable route and runtime identity.

title: Rework device page top panel into navigation panel
description:
Replace the current device-page top card with a navigation-focused panel.

Scope:
- rename the top panel concept to navigation panel
- move high-level navigation/status content there instead of device detail content
- place the txing logo from https://txing.dev branding on the left
- move the sign-in/sign-out control to the right side of the navigation panel
- place the current shadow timestamp immediately left of the auth control
- remove the separate open-video-route button from the device page

Constraint:
- keep the existing device detail/control panel below the navigation panel
design: The navigation panel should emphasize orientation and session controls, not device detail. The timestamp and auth control should be right-aligned, while branding and breadcrumb live on the left.
notes: Implemented by commits 475e2f3 and 39adea1. The device page top card was reworked into a navigation panel, auth/timestamp moved into the nav chrome, and the separate open-video button was removed.

title: Stop device preflight from requiring DescribeThing
description:
The shared aws.check device-scope preflight still calls IoT DescribeThing and validates town/rig attributes. The board runtime no longer needs DescribeThing after video URL/channel derivation moved out of board runtime config and shadow metadata. On deployed stacks, the device runtime role lacks iot:DescribeThing, so just board::check fails even though real runtime prerequisites are satisfied.

Scope:
- update device-scope aws.check to stop probing DescribeThing and registry town/rig attributes
- keep GetThingShadow, DescribeEndpoint, and KinesisVideo signaling checks intact
- adjust tests to match the reduced device runtime prerequisite set

Constraint:
- do not expand device IAM just to satisfy preflight for an unused control-plane call
design: Board runtime currently derives the video channel from THING_NAME and does not need registry reads. The check should validate only actual runtime dependencies for device scope.

title: Compute device video URLs from route schema
description:
Replace the current config/stack-driven board video viewer URL contract with a computed route contract derived from the current device assignment. The viewer URL must no longer come from BOARD_VIDEO_VIEWER_URL or the CloudFormation BoardVideoViewerUrl output as the source of truth.

Scope:
- define a canonical video URL schema for device-scoped video pages
- compute that URL from the selected or assigned town, rig, and device identity instead of storing a full URL in thing attributes or config files
- update the board/web integration so the published video session metadata follows the computed schema
- document the final video URL schema alongside the existing /<town>/<rig>/<device> contract

Constraints:
- treat URLs as derived values, not registry facts
- keep stable identity in device_id and current assignment in town/rig attributes
- avoid making the viewer URL depend on per-host config files except for the web origin/base URL
- preserve direct deep-linkability for device video views
design: Preferred direction: make the video route device-scoped, likely /<town>/<rig>/<device>/video, and derive it from current assignment metadata plus the deployed web origin. The board should publish video channel/session facts, not own the canonical operator URL. Documentation should clearly define both the device detail route and the video route.
notes: Implemented by commit 475e2f3. Device video URLs are now derived from the canonical route schema instead of config or stack outputs, and the board/web contract uses computed device-scoped video routing.

title: Build route-driven rig and device browsing UI
description: Replace the single-device SPA entry flow with compact route-driven pages for /<town>, /<town>/<rig>, and /<town>/<rig>/<device>. Add route parse/build helpers, browser history navigation, breadcrumbs, unsupported-town handling, and town/rig/device page states. Only create shadow sessions on the device route and keep the current device detail UI behavior unchanged once a device is selected.
notes: Implemented by commits 475e2f3 and 39adea1. The SPA now supports /<town>, /<town>/<rig>, and /<town>/<rig>/<device> with route helpers, browser navigation, unsupported-town handling, and device-only shadow session startup.

title: Add browser AWS catalog access for rig and device listing
description: Add SPA-side AWS IoT catalog access using the existing Cognito-backed credential path. List rig thing groups for the configured town deployment, list things in a selected rig group, validate route-selected devices against the selected rig, and extend browser IAM permissions in the shared AWS stack for the required list/describe calls. Keep /video and existing shadow/Sparkplug publish behavior intact.
notes: Implemented by commits 475e2f3 and 39adea1. The SPA now lists rig thing groups and rig devices through browser-side AWS IoT catalog access, validates route-selected devices against rig membership, and includes the required browser IAM permissions.

title: Update web config contracts and regression coverage for drilldown
description: Make VITE_DEVICE_THING_NAME and VITE_SPARKPLUG_EDGE_NODE_ID optional for normal SPA boot, keep VITE_SPARKPLUG_GROUP_ID as the configured town identifier, update README/env behavior, and add regression coverage for routes, navigation, config, and catalog flows. Ensure invalid routes fail cleanly instead of falling back to unit-local.
notes: Implemented by the route-driven web drilldown work and follow-up UI refinements in commits 475e2f3 and 39adea1. Web config now treats VITE_SPARKPLUG_GROUP_ID as required town scope while device/rig selections are route-driven, with regression coverage for config and route behavior.

title: Implement dynamic town/rig/device drilldown in web SPA
description: Approved implementation summary: convert the SPA from a single hardcoded device view into a route-driven browser for one configured town. Use compact paths /<town>, /<town>/<rig>, and /<town>/<rig>/<device>, while preserving /video. The town page lists rig thing groups, the rig page lists device IDs in the selected group, and the device page reuses the existing shadow/Sparkplug/cmd_vel UI. Keep this phase SPA-only with Cognito-backed AWS calls, no backend service, and no extra attributes in list pages. Route-selected rig is the Sparkplug edge node ID and route-selected device is the AWS IoT thing name. Extend browser IAM permissions as needed and cover route/catalog/config behavior with tests.
notes: All approved drilldown work has been implemented and delivered across commits 475e2f3, 39adea1, and 1e06da4, with the earlier in-progress child tasks now manually reconciled and closed. Remaining open follow-up txing-pfs is separate from this epic.

title: Use configured device id in web env generation
description: The SPA currently defaults VITE_DEVICE_THING_NAME to unit-local because web::write-env hardcodes the placeholder instead of deriving THING_NAME from device scope config. Make the no-argument web env generation path load the configured board device id from config/board.env via _project-aws-env device, keep explicit overrides possible, and update any docs/tests that assert the old placeholder output.

title: Fix browser IoT shadow permissions for dynamic device ids
description: The web SPA now targets registered device ids like unit-xxxxxx, but the CloudFormation policies attached to browser/Cognito identities still allow only /things/txing/shadow/* topics. Update the shared AWS stack so authenticated browser access can publish/subscribe/receive classic shadow topics for registered devices, and keep cmd_vel access aligned with the multi-device model.

title: Use configured device id in aws::check
description: The aggregate just aws::check recipe still defaults thing_name to unit-local instead of reading THING_NAME from the device scope env. Make the no-argument path derive the device thing name from config/board.env via _project-aws-env device, use it consistently for the device Python preflight and the town-level no-op UpdateThing probe, and keep explicit overrides working.

title: Reduce thing-type searchable attributes to AWS limit
description: The current device registration path tries to create thing types with six searchableAttributes and fails because AWS IoT allows only three searchable attributes per thing type. Rework the clean registration model so the unit thing type exposes only the minimum searchable attributes required by the product requirements and rig fleet behavior. Keep rig and bleDeviceId searchable as required, include one additional attribute if needed to exclude legacy things cleanly, and simplify the dynamic rig-group query to rely only on supported searchable attributes.

title: Make device registry attributes searchable and exclude legacy rig members
description: The new device registration flow creates thing types without searchableAttributes, so core registry attributes like rig and bleDeviceId appear unsearchable. The rig dynamic group query is also too broad and still admits the legacy txing thing, which then gets skipped at runtime because it lacks required registry attributes. Update the shared AWS stack/indexing configuration and device registry so the registered-device attributes are searchable, and tighten the rig dynamic thing-group query so it only includes fully registered devices.

title: Refresh docs and regression coverage for device identity rework
description: Update docs, examples, and tests to reflect the new txing platform/device model, stable device IDs, registration/reassignment flows, devices/template, and the devices/unit extraction. Add regression tests for registration, reassignment, adapter loading, and unit runtime/web behavior.

title: Rename generic runtime interfaces to device-oriented names
description: Rename generic env/runtime identifiers that still encode the old concrete txing device, including AWS_TXING_PROFILE to AWS_DEVICE_PROFILE and VITE_TXING_THING_NAME to VITE_DEVICE_THING_NAME, while preserving current unit behavior behind the new device-type adapters.

title: Make shared AWS stack generic for registered devices
description: Remove the fixed Thing and fixed board-video channel from the shared CloudFormation stack and make IAM/policies generic for registered devices, per-thing shadow access, per-device cmd_vel topics, and per-device KVS access.

title: Implement device registration and reassignment flows
description: Add manifest-driven device registration and reassignment flows. Registration must create stable device IDs in the form <device_type>-<short_id>, write registry attributes town/rig/deviceType/deviceName/shortId, seed the shadow only when missing, and create unit auxiliary resources such as the per-device KVS channel.

title: Create device-type layout and extract unit/bot implementation
description: Create the new devices/ layout, move the current concrete implementation into devices/unit, add devices/unit/manifest.toml, and keep top-level rig/web/shared generic. This task covers the initial structural move plus the unit-specific adapter extraction needed for rig and web.

title: Txing platform/device identity rework
description:
Approved implementation plan for the txing platform reorganization.

Summary
- Treat txing as the platform/repo concept only.
- The current real implementation becomes device_type=unit with device_name=bot.
- Add a first-class stable device_id as the AWS IoT Thing name, independent of town and rig.
- Introduce a type-first devices/ layout with devices/unit for the current implementation and devices/template as a scaffold-only reference.
- Replace the fixed single-device AWS model with manifest-driven registration and reassignment flows.

Scope
- Move current concrete code and contracts under devices/unit.
- Keep top-level rig/web/shared as generic platform code.
- Add manifest-driven device registration, reassignment, and auxiliary resource creation.
- Remove fixed thing/channel resources from the shared AWS stack and make policies generic for registered devices.
- Rename generic env/runtime identifiers to device-oriented names.

Constraints
- Clean break migration; no compatibility layer for fixed txing resources.
- Stable device IDs must not encode town or rig.
- devices/template is scaffold-only and not a valid runtime device type.
- Keep current unit semantics unchanged behind adapters, including Sparkplug device ID, cmd_vel topic, and bleDeviceId ownership.

Implementation intent
- Registration creates device IDs in the form <device_type>-<short_id> using a locally generated 6-character lowercase base36 suffix, collision-checked against existing things.
- Registry attributes include town, rig, deviceType, deviceName, and shortId.
- Unit registration also ensures a KVS signaling channel named <device_id>-board-video.
- Runtime/device discovery ignores folders without manifest.toml.

Acceptance
- devices/unit contains the current implementation, devices/template is copyable scaffold-only, registration/reassignment flows work, and unit runtime/web behavior still passes regression tests.

title: aws+web/ allow per-session browser MQTT client ids in web IoT policy
description:
Scope
The browser web admin now uses a unique per-session MQTT client ID derived from the Cognito identity ID to avoid client-ID collisions across tabs and StrictMode remounts. The CloudFormation web IoT policy still authorizes only the exact identity ID as the client resource, so connects are refused as not authorized.

Constraints
- Keep the Cognito identity ID as the stable policy anchor.
- Preserve the current shadow and Sparkplug topic permissions.
- Keep the fix limited to the web admin IoT policy and related docs.

Intended approach
Update the web admin IoT connect policy resource to allow the identity ID plus the browser session suffix pattern, then document the per-session client-ID behavior and note that a stack deploy is required for the policy change to take effect.

title: web/ use unique MQTT client ids for browser shadow sessions
description:
Scope
The browser Thing Shadow session currently uses the Cognito IdentityId directly as the AWS IoT MQTT client ID. In React StrictMode, during fast reloads, or with multiple tabs, the browser can open overlapping sessions with the same client ID and AWS IoT disconnects one of them.

Constraints
- Keep Cognito identity lookup for policy attachment and credentials.
- Do not change the shadow topic layout or server-side policy assumptions.
- Keep the fix scoped to the browser shadow session.

Intended approach
Derive a unique per-session MQTT client ID from the Cognito identity ID plus a local random/session suffix, then add or update tests around the helper if web tests exist.

title: beads/ investigate duplicate issue creation and restore clean tracker state
description:
Scope
Investigate why recent bd operations created duplicate epic trees and modified .beads/issues.jsonl unexpectedly, verify whether this is a known regression in the current bd release, and restore the tracker to the intended clean state.

Constraints
- Preserve the single intended epic tree and remove only unintended duplicates.
- Keep a clear audit trail in Beads for any cleanup operations.
- Check upstream release notes or issue tracker before assuming local misuse.

Intended approach
Inspect local Beads state, compare with the current upstream bd version behavior, identify the cause of duplicate creation, then clean the duplicated issues and leave the repository tracker consistent again.

title: docs/ retire temporary Phase 1 task lists and provisional Phase 1 docs
description:
Scope
After the open topics above are resolved, remove or rewrite the temporary Phase 1 task lists and any now-provisional Phase 1 design or planning documents so the repo only keeps stable implementation-facing documentation.

Constraints
- Do not start this cleanup until the other Phase 1 topic issues are settled.
- Preserve durable contracts and operational guidance, but remove stale checklists and transitional planning language.
- The end state should make it obvious what is implemented now without reading historical Phase 1 plans.

Intended approach
Consolidate durable behavior into stable docs such as the shadow contract, rig/device contract, README files, and any retained design notes, then delete or rewrite the temporary phase1 task files and related provisional Phase 1 artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Temporary Phase 1 task/checklist docs are removed or rewritten into stable docs, and the repo no longer carries leftover Phase 1 planning artifacts.

title: docs/ reconcile board-video design docs with the shipped native sender
description:
Scope
Current board-video docs have drift: some text says the repo does not embed the media-pipeline implementation directly, while other repo docs say the native sender is shipped in-tree. Resolve that contradiction.

Constraints
- Reflect the actual current implementation, not an older planned state.
- Keep the distinction between the Python supervisor and the native sender executable clear.
- Avoid broad architectural changes; this is about making the documented architecture honest.

Intended approach
Audit the board README, Phase 1 design docs, and related guidance, then rewrite them to one stable statement that matches the current code layout and runtime responsibilities.
acceptance_criteria: README, design docs, and other repo guidance no longer contradict each other about whether the repo ships the native sender or only supervises an external implementation.

title: ops+docs/ close the remaining Phase 1 board-video field-validation work
description:
Scope
The board-video Phase 1 tasks still leave open latency, jitter and stall behavior, directional-control quality, and the decision on whether a second direct operator path should be reconsidered. Close that gap in a way that leaves the repo consistent.

Constraints
- Do not invent measurements.
- If field validation cannot be completed directly from the repo, convert the open-ended Phase 1 wording into an explicit follow-on process or artifact instead of leaving checklist items hanging.
- Keep the decision trail visible in-repo so future work is grounded in evidence.

Intended approach
Gather existing evidence and determine whether this issue should land measured results, a durable field-test report or process artifact, or a documented move of the validation work out of Phase 1, then update the docs accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: The repo no longer has unresolved Phase 1 field-test tasks; either real evidence is recorded or the validation work is explicitly moved out of Phase 1 and documented consistently.

title: board/ close the remaining Phase 1 single-operator scope gap
description:
Scope
The board-video Phase 1 tasks still leave initial operator scope to one human operator open. Resolve whether Phase 1 requires actual enforcement, only a documented assumption, or a narrower statement.

Constraints
- Preserve the current AWS KVS viewer path unless a minimal guardrail is needed to make the contract honest.
- Do not turn this task into general multiviewer feature work.
- Keep board.video.viewerConnected semantics explicit if they remain only best-effort.

Intended approach
Audit the sender and browser behavior, choose the stable contract for Phase 1 operator scope, implement any minimal guardrails if needed, and update docs/tests to remove the open-ended wording.
acceptance_criteria: The repo explicitly defines whether Phase 1 single-operator scope is enforced or only assumed, and code/docs/tests match that choice.

title: rig/ align rig.redcon node metric docs with actual NBIRTH/NDATA behavior
description:
Scope
The Phase 1 Sparkplug docs say rig.redcon is published through NBIRTH and NDATA, but the rig runtime only emits NBIRTH for the node metric today. Resolve that mismatch.

Constraints
- Keep the current meaning of rig.redcon=1 when the rig lifecycle service is up.
- Do not reintroduce AWS IoT thing or shadow reflection for rig.
- Prefer the smallest consistent contract change that keeps runtime behavior and docs aligned.

Intended approach
Inspect the existing node-metric publish path, decide whether to implement NDATA publication or narrow the contract to NBIRTH-only behavior, then update runtime, tests, and docs together.
acceptance_criteria: The repo has one clear contract for rig.redcon publication, and the runtime/tests/docs all match it.

title: Finish Phase 1 closure and repo consistency cleanup
description:
Goal
Finish the remaining Sparkplug lifecycle and board-video Phase 1 work so the repository reflects the actual completed behavior and no temporary Phase 1 task lists or provisional Phase 1 planning docs remain.

Scope
- Resolve each remaining open Phase 1 topic with implementation or documentation changes.
- Close the Sparkplug rig.redcon NBIRTH/NDATA gap.
- Close the remaining board-video placeholders: single-operator scope, native-client expansion stance, ML/cloud consume path, field validation, and doc drift around the shipped native sender.
- Retire or rewrite temporary Phase 1 task/design docs once final behavior is settled.

Constraints
- End state must be repo-consistent: code, tests, README, schema, and docs agree.
- Ownership rules stay intact unless explicitly revised.
- Documentation-only resolution is acceptable if explicit and reflected in repo.
- Do not leave stale Phase 1 task checklists or unresolved Phase 1 placeholders in-tree.

Intended approach
Create one child issue per open topic, resolve each topic from current code evidence, then do a final cleanup pass that removes or rewrites temporary Phase 1 planning docs into stable documentation.
acceptance_criteria: All remaining Phase 1 topics are resolved by implementation or documentation changes; code/tests/docs agree; temporary Phase 1 task lists and provisional Phase 1 docs are removed or rewritten into stable docs.

title: board split board runtime config from aws env
description: Board runtime should load a separate board env file for board-local defaults, keep aws env focused on AWS and rig defaults, and add configurable cmd_vel raw min and max mapping without changing raw helper semantics.

title: board video sender should inherit resolved AWS credentials from parent
description:
## Problem
 resolves AWS credentials and connects to AWS IoT successfully, but the supervised  child still re-resolves the  profile and fails under systemd with .

## Scope
- Stop relying on child-side profile resolution when the parent already has working credentials.
- Pass a resolved credential snapshot from  into the video sender supervisor/child.
- Keep standalone  support for manual runs.
- Add regression tests for supervisor spawn env and direct child startup with explicit credentials.

## Constraints
- Do not change MQTT/shadow contracts.
- Preserve native sender credential injection behavior.

title: board install-service should use EnvironmentFile defaults again
description:
## Problem
 now bakes resolved AWS env into  lines by default. That overrides  and makes the generated unit harder to reason about and update.

## Scope
- Simplify  so  remains the default source of runtime configuration.
- Keep explicit install-time overrides as  lines only when they were actually passed.
- Preserve  support for the motor stack.
- Update tests/docs to match the simpler model.

## Constraints
- Do not change MQTT/shadow contracts.
- Do not revert unrelated in-progress  changes.

title: board video sender spawn should not depend on implicit inherited AWS env
description:
## Problem
 can connect to AWS IoT, then fail when the supervised  child starts under systemd with . The child currently relies on inherited environment and working directory instead of getting an explicit normalized AWS spawn environment.

## Scope
- Normalize AWS profile/config/credentials env before spawning .
- Pass explicit  to the child so repo-relative config paths resolve consistently.
- Add regression tests around supervisor spawn env/cwd.

## Constraints
- Do not change MQTT/shadow contracts.
- Keep board video startup behavior the same apart from making the child spawn deterministic.
notes: Made board video sender spawn deterministic: normalize AWS_PROFILE/AWS_DEFAULT_PROFILE and credential/config file paths into an explicit child env, and pass explicit cwd to the child subprocess. Added regression tests covering normalized spawn env/cwd. Board tests pass: unittest discover (57 tests). Pending device verification and commit.

title: board service fails video sender when txing profile is not explicitly exported
description:
## Problem
 can start and connect AWS IoT, but  fails with  after recent service/env changes.

## Scope
- Ensure install-service always propagates resolved AWS runtime environment (, , , , ) into systemd unit env, not only when explicit CLI args are passed.
- Keep existing EnvironmentFile behavior.
- Add/adjust tests to lock this behavior.

## Constraints
- No MQTT/shadow contract changes.
- No manual systemctl edits required after .
notes: Patched board::install-service to always emit AWS_REGION/AWS_PROFILE/AWS_DEFAULT_PROFILE/AWS_TXING_PROFILE/AWS_SHARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE/AWS_CONFIG_FILE into board.service Environment lines using resolved values from _project-aws-env; kept board-specific overrides optional. Updated board/tests/test_shadow_control.py assertions and README service docs. Board unit tests pass: unittest discover (55 tests). Pending runtime verification on device and commit.

title: board/ fix lgpio notify pipe path under systemd
description: board runtime fails at startup with xCreatePipe permission/path error for .lgd-nfy0 when lgpio initializes under systemd. Fix by setting a stable writable LG_WD before importing gpiozero LGPIO backend, and document override option.
design: Set default LG_WD=/tmp/txing-lgpio when unset, create dir if needed, then import/use LGPIOFactory. Keep override behavior if LG_WD is explicitly configured. Update board README troubleshooting/runtime notes.

title: board/ add raw left-right motor test helper and just recipe
description:
Add a board-local direct motor test path for bring-up and tuning.

Scope:
- Add a helper/CLI that applies raw left/right motor values directly in the `[-480, 480]` range.
- Reuse the same DRV8835 backend as the main board runtime instead of a separate GPIO path.
- Add a `just` entrypoint for running this manual test path from the repo root.
- The direct test path should bypass MQTT and kinematic mixing; it is for raw backend validation.
design:
Concrete approach:
- Add a small Python entrypoint that accepts raw `left` and `right` values, validates range, applies them through the shared backend, and stops on exit.
- Add a `just board::...` recipe that forwards the raw values into that helper so bring-up does not require composing Python commands manually.
- Keep the command surface focused on raw left/right motor testing rather than simulating a `Twist` payload.

title: board/ add native DRV8835 backend with hardware PWM
description:
Add a repo-owned DRV8835 backend for the board runtime.

Scope:
- Implement the real motor driver adapter used by `CmdVelController`.
- Use hardware PWM for speed and Python-native GPIO line control for direction.
- Keep the stock Pololu shield pin mapping as the default in v1.
- Add runtime configuration for inversion and the hardware/PWM parameters needed to run safely on Raspberry Pi OS.
- Keep shutdown, disconnect, and watchdog stop behavior safe.
design:
Concrete approach:
- Add a motor backend module that exposes the same `setSpeeds(left, right)` shape expected by the existing controller.
- Keep the controller's public/output scale at `[-100, 100]` and introduce an internal mapping layer to raw `[-480, 480]` hardware units.
- Use kernel PWM channels for the speed pins and native Python GPIO for the direction pins; do not depend on WiringPi.
- Ensure close/error handling drives both motors to zero and releases claimed resources cleanly.

title: board/ native DRV8835 motor control and raw test path
description:
Implement real board-side motor control for the Pololu DRV8835 Dual Motor Driver Kit on the txing board runtime.

Plan summary:
- Replace the current stub-only motor backend with a repo-owned native Python backend.
- Do not use the WiringPi-based Pololu Python package.
- Match the Pololu behavior closely by using hardware PWM for the speed channels and native Python GPIO control for the direction channels, following current Raspberry Pi OS guidance.
- Keep the existing MQTT `txing/board/cmd_vel` contract and existing shadow `reported.board.drive.*` contract unchanged.
- Preserve the repo-visible drive scale as signed percent effort `[-100, 100]`, but map that scale internally to the DRV8835 raw range `[-480, 480]`.
- Assume the stock Pololu shield pin mapping in v1.
- Add a manual board-local test path and `just` recipe for setting raw left/right motor values directly in the `[-480, 480]` range.
- Add the required docs, tests, and boot/runtime prerequisites for the PWM path.
design:
Implementation intent:
- Use Linux hardware PWM for the two speed outputs and Python-native GPIO control for the two direction pins.
- Default to the stock Pololu mapping: GPIO12/GPIO13 as PWM outputs and GPIO5/GPIO6 as direction pins.
- Scale percent effort from `CmdVelController` into raw DRV8835 units before hitting hardware.
- Add explicit config for inversion/calibration instead of baking chassis direction assumptions into code.
- Add a small direct motor-test helper and wire it into `just` so raw values can be applied without MQTT.
- Force stop on close/error paths and keep tests hardware-free with fakes around the PWM/GPIO adapters.

title: Handle browser ICE candidate edge cases in KVS video path
description:
Scope:
- web viewer should not signal empty ICE candidates that the native sender cannot parse
- native board sender should tolerate ignorable browser ICE candidate parse failures, including empty candidate payloads and mDNS-obfuscated host candidates, without tearing down the session

Constraints:
- keep current AWS KVS signaling architecture
- keep changes scoped to board and web
- prefer compatibility with modern browser trickle ICE behavior over strict fatal handling

Intended approach:
- filter outgoing viewer ICE candidates to only send non-empty candidate strings
- relax board remote ICE candidate handling so known ignorable candidate parsing failures are logged and skipped instead of treated as fatal
- add targeted debug logging for skipped candidates
notes:
Implemented viewer-side empty ICE candidate filtering and board-side remote candidate tolerance for empty/mDNS-obfuscated candidates. Verified with bun run build in web and syntax-only compile of board/kvs_session_real.cpp against the local AWS SDK headers.
Committed on main as 3682256: Handle browser ICE candidate edge cases. Issue remains open pending runtime validation in browser/hardware.
close_reason: Closed at user request. Implemented in commit 3682256; issue remained open only for follow-up validation.

title: Move SPA IoT endpoint to runtime discovery
description:
Remove `VITE_IOT_DATA_ENDPOINT` from config/docs/env generation.
Add browser-side IoT `DescribeEndpoint` resolution with caching.
Update shadow session/App wiring so endpoint is resolved internally at runtime.

title: Grant browser identities DescribeEndpoint and verify
description:
Add `iot:DescribeEndpoint` to the authenticated Cognito identity role in `shared/aws/template.yaml`.
Keep MQTT topic permissions unchanged.
Verify web runtime works with no precomputed endpoint env.

title: Remove endpoint file from root/shared shell flow
description:
Remove `AWS_ENDPOINT_FILE` from root `_project-aws-env`, config examples, and shell recipe callsites.
Delete `certs/iot-data-ats.endpoint`.
Remove `shared/aws::endpoint`.
Make `aws::check`, `aws::shadow`, and `aws::shadow-reset` resolve Data-ATS directly via `DescribeEndpoint`.

title: Complete IoT endpoint cleanup and AWS connection consolidation
description:
Remove `iot-data-ats.endpoint` and `AWS_ENDPOINT_FILE` from repo/runtime contracts.
Keep `rig` and `board` on runtime endpoint discovery through `shared/aws/python`.
Remove remaining Python AWS compatibility shims that only re-export shared code.
Add browser runtime `DescribeEndpoint` and required permission on Cognito browser identities.
Preserve current rig/board behavior and web shadow/MQTT functionality while removing file-based endpoint plumbing.

title: Native KVS sender still requires explicit CA file
description: Investigate board native KVS sender still hitting readCACertificate() and hanging under board.service after system trust-store cleanup. Determine where CA file expectation still comes from in kvs_master or vendored aws-kvs-webrtc-sdk build, fix the runtime/build so board video sender reaches ready state without repo-local CA handling, and keep logs/tests aligned.

title: Debug board video sender startup timeout with no child logs
description: Investigate board.service startup timeout where shadow_control waits 30s for video sender readiness but no txing-board-video-sender child logs appear. Determine whether board-video-sender is not launching the native sender, is blocked before child startup, or is failing to surface state/output correctly. Fix the runtime path so startup failures are observable and the service either reaches ready or fails with actionable logs.

title: Make board native SDK build use system OpenSSL explicitly
description: Tighten board/native AWS KVS SDK build wiring so the checked-out latest SDK and its libkvsCommonLws dependency both use system-provided OpenSSL/libcrypto on Linux. Avoid misleading rebuild behavior/noise that looks like vendored OpenSSL work, and reduce upstream OpenSSL 3 deprecation warning spam in the local SDK compile path without changing txing runtime behavior.

title: Use Debian CA only for board video and shared AWS flows
description: Standardize board video TLS on the Debian trust store only. Remove board CA override knobs and repo-local cert.pem discovery, set the board-local AWS KVS SDK build to use /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt, and remove the shared/aws AmazonRootCA1 bootstrap helper and its use in legacy bootstrap flows. Update docs and tests accordingly.

title: Strip inherited TLS CA env from board video sender
description: board.video_sender currently copies parent TLS CA env into the native kvs_master child, which can force readCACertificate() failures even when the intended default is to use the native OpenSSL trust store. Remove inherited SSL_CERT_FILE/AWS_KVS_CACERT_PATH from the child unless BOARD_VIDEO_CA_FILE or --ca-file is explicitly set.

title: Fix native sender TLS root discovery in kvs_master
description: Investigate and fix the native board KVS sender TLS trust path. The native sender currently reports unable to get local issuer certificate when talking to AWS signaling endpoints. Make CA root discovery robust inside kvs_master itself and add explicit logging/failure messages for the resolved CA path.

title: Fix native board video sender TLS CA handling
description: Investigate and fix board video sender native TLS trust so the KVS sender can validate AWS signaling endpoint certificates on deployed txing hosts. Keep the current AWS profile/credential model. Validate CA bundle discovery and what gets passed into the native sender/runtime.

title: Phase 4 integration docs and verification
description: Update Phase 4 documentation and run focused verification across shared contract tests, board tests, web tests, and native KVS tests.
design: Document optional capability combinations and MQTT fallback invariant. Leave manual hardware/browser acceptance steps explicit.

title: Center debug button and remove manual detail nav toggle
description: The debug button icon is visually off-center in the video/status control, and the navigation panel still shows manual detail buttons even though detail panels are now driven by route/REDCON state.
design: Adjust the debug button/icon CSS so the glyph is centered, remove the Sparkplug detail toggle from the navigation panel at all levels, and update affected render tests.

title: web: integrate bot indicators into video overlay and relocate debug controls
description:
Scope:
- web/ and devices/unit/web/ only
- Move the bot header indicators (left/right track, BOT label, connectivity, battery) into the video area as a transparent integrated overlay.
- Move Load Shadow into the debug panel header, positioned left of the last-shadow timestamp.
- Replace the current text debug toggle in the bot panel with a left-side video overlay control in a style aligned with the connectivity/power indicators, using a debug icon.

Constraints:
- Preserve existing bot/video functionality and debug toggle behavior.
- Keep the standalone device video route usable even without the bot overlay.
- Add focused tests for the updated bot panel/debug panel rendering.

title: web: simplify avatar menu and move device actions into bot panel
description:
Scope:
- web/ only
- Keep the avatar menu right-aligned to the navigation panel, always above surrounding content, and limit its actions to Show/Hide Session Log and Sign Off.
- Move the device-specific actions currently in the avatar menu (Load Shadow and Enable/Disable Debug) into the bot detail panel as two buttons for now.

Constraints:
- Preserve current session-log toggle behavior and sign-off behavior.
- Keep the new device action buttons scoped to the device panel.
- Add focused test coverage for the updated menu and device panel actions.

title: web: suppress expected MCP timeout after redcon teardown
description:
Scope:
- web/ only
- When a device transitions out of the MCP/video-capable state (for example from REDCON 1 to 4), in-flight MCP robot-state requests can time out after the board-side MCP service closes. Do not surface that expected teardown as a session log/runtime error.

Constraints:
- Preserve real MCP error reporting while the device is still in the MCP/video-capable state.
- Keep the fix scoped to expected teardown/unavailable cases.
- Add focused test coverage for the MCP error classification used by the web app.

title: web: auto-open bot detail should expand board video
description:
Scope:
- web/ only
- Fix the device detail auto-open path so when a device transitions to REDCON 1 and the detail panel opens automatically, the board video viewer is expanded and initialized the same way as when the user opens the panel manually.

Constraints:
- Preserve manual detail-button toggle behavior.
- Do not change the REDCON 1 gating for device details.
- Add focused test coverage for the auto-open state behavior.

title: web: auto-open level detail panels and simplify detail rows
description:
Scope:
- web/ only
- Auto-open detail panels when drilling down into town and rig routes.
- Auto-open the device detail panel when the device reaches REDCON 1.
- Simplify detail panel card rows so each card shows one centered line in the form short-id: name, without extra type labels like Rig or Device.

Constraints:
- Preserve existing route structure and REDCON control behavior.
- Keep device detail access gated by REDCON 1.
- Update or add focused tests for panel state and card rendering.
notes: Extended scope: include the object id in session log entries so runtime messages can be traced to the relevant town/rig/device.

title: BLE device should send confirmation that it is up after wake
Add Sparkplug details toggle for town and rig routes
description: Extend the web Sparkplug strip so town and rig routes also show a details button similar to the bot device-details button. The button should follow the same strip enablement pattern based on the row REDCON posture, and toggle visibility of the current route content: town route toggles the rig list, rig route toggles the device list, and device route keeps toggling the device panel. Keep the navigation strip as the route-independent lifecycle surface and move content visibility control into the same details-button concept across all three levels.

title: Keep BLE session alive for awake device after REDCON convergence
description: Rig fleet manager currently disconnects the active BLE session as soon as desired.redcon converges. In single-device mode this violates the intended hybrid BLE model where sleep uses rendezvous and awake keeps a live BLE connection. After wake, the rig falls back to advertisement freshness, can miss advertisements, marks BLE offline, drops REDCON to 4, and the web control path fails even though the MCU remains awake. Keep the BLE session alive while the device is awake, and tighten wake confirmation/read timeout handling so a stuck GATT read cannot leave the bridge in command_sent for minutes.

title: Convert bot Sparkplug strip to direct REDCON control
description: Update the web Sparkplug strip so bot-level lifecycle control is driven directly by REDCON state selection instead of the On/Off switch. Remove the BOT and REDCON labels around the redcon indicator, show a clear horizontal 4-state REDCON control with connecting lines, allow direct manual target selection of REDCON 1-4 on bot routes, remove visible mV text from the battery metric while keeping tooltip/aria detail, and keep the strip horizontally aligned in the navigation bar.
acceptance_criteria:
- Bot route Sparkplug strip has no BOT or REDCON labels
- Bot route shows a clear horizontal 4-state REDCON control with connecting lines
- REDCON states are directly clickable to publish the selected REDCON target
- Current REDCON is visibly active and current target cannot be re-selected while pending
- Visible battery text is removed and tooltip remains
- Focused web tests and build pass

title: Require rig ack before web reports Sparkplug redcon success
description: The navigation Sparkplug On control currently shows a success notification as soon as the browser publish call resolves. That is only local MQTT publish completion, not end-to-end rig receipt. Tighten the web command flow so DCMD.redcon is only treated as successful after the rig reflects desired.redcon into the device shadow. If no ack arrives within a short timeout, report an error instead of a success toast.
acceptance_criteria:
- Pressing On does not show success unless desired.redcon is reflected by rig into shadow
- Missing rig ack surfaces as an error
- Existing Sparkplug command flow still works for wake and sleep
- Web tests cover the ack wait behavior
